attacking_style: Not a first-attack rider; excels in reactive counter-attacks and positioning for reduced sprints from GC groups after penultimate climbs. Seizes bonus seconds opportunistically when form is good.
team_tactics: Flexible role player: works as domestique when team prioritizes another leader, but given freedom when co-leading. Team often lacks numbers to control finales, limiting setup options.
terrain_preference: Stage races with multiple categorized climbs where durability and recovery between efforts matter. Strong in reduced sprints after selection. Time trials provide GC gain opportunities.
vulnerability: Exposed when racing defensively without numerical support. Mechanicals at critical moments have cost time. Can appear dropped on earlier climbs before recovering, suggesting pacing management issues.
key_patterns: Counter-attacks when others hesitate rather than initiating moves (2020 Tirreno, 2020 Lombardia pattern)
Recovers after appearing dropped on climbs to finish with GC group (2023 Vuelta durability)
Actively hunts bonus seconds when competitive for top-10 GC (2023 Vuelta, positioning behavior)
Willing domestique despite podium capability when team strategy requires it (2020 Lombardia sacrifice)
Peaks well for stage race blocks: Romandie-Suisse 2022 trajectory toward Grand Tours
Wins reduced sprints after penultimate climb selections, not final climb attacks (Suisse 2022)
form_trajectory: neutral: Limited 2023 data shows competitiveness but supporting role. 2022 peaked well (Romandie/Suisse wins). Mixed signals between leadership and domestique duties.
built_from: 2023 La Vuelta Ciclista a Espana Stage 9
